programme.title: Location, Location, Location

Meanwhile, Marc Harris and Wayne Williams reveal their competitive streak during an afternoon of horse riding(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to The Lion Hotel in Pontypool for the third day of the contest, where manager Marc Harris and head chef Wayne Williams aim to deliver a high end service. Although some of the guests are impressed by the rooms, Ray and Fay find it too small for the price, and simmering tensions between some of the competitors leads to a clash over dinner. At breakfast, Wayne's food comes under fire, but it is David and Donna's feedback about finding a nail in their room that really rattles their hosts(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final guest house of the week is the New Inn, Talgarth, where ex-naval officer Damon Lewis is the sole captain of his ship. Wayne and Marc are determined to be brutally honest, and Donna and David are forced to work closely with their rivals Fay and Ray during an afternoon team-building exercise. Over dinner, Damon admits that taking on the B&B, pub and restaurant all by himself may have not been the best decision - as proved when he has trouble running a solo breakfast service(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time, with Fay and Ray from Shiralee B&B wanting to find out why David and Donna were so critical of their cleanliness. The group members continue to air their grievances as they find out what they've been paid, before the winner is revealed(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trucker Tony Houston holds the first dinner party in Clacton-on-Sea, Essex, hoping his menu of scallops on black pudding with pea puree, and sea bass with prawn mash will impress his fellow diners. However, if his food fails to live up to his expectations, he anticipates the whole ambience and presentation of the evening will secure him the £1,000 cash prize(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Anthony Street hosts the second day in Essex, and has his work cut out trying to impress the diners after displaying some unusual table manners the previous night. However, he hopes his passion for quirky entertainment will be enough to win them over(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Driving instructor Claire Kelly hosts the third dinner party in Essex. Her devil-may-care approach does nothing to dampen her confidence, and she is convinced hers will be the winning night. Even if her chaotic cooking style and haphazard evening fail to win over the guests, the mother-of-two has some special entertainment lined up to gain a few extra points(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Entrepreneur and foraging fan Richard Harris hosts the fourth night of the competition in the Clacton-on-Sea area of Essex. Not everybody is impressed when they discover he has had help, and Claire Kelly accuses him of being a food snob. However, he hopes a performance by the local church choir will help improve the atmosphere(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teacher Kerrie Brinkley-Whittington hosts the final dinner party in Essex, and uses a few props to help her night go with a swing. However, she is soon under fire from entrepreneur Richard, who is not amused to be asked to contribute more to group conversations, and when trucker Tony makes an emergency run to the garden, it looks as if the rhubarb dessert could be just as controversial. At the end of the evening, the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedics help a 24-year-old man who is having an epileptic seizure in a supermarket, while an ambulance crew treats an elderly woman whose nose will not stop bleeding(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-nine-year-old Carole is brought in with signs of a stroke, and neurologist Dr Mahinda opts for a treatment that only has a slim chance of success. A 77-year-old man with diabetes has an infection in his toes, and doctors consider amputating his foot to stop it spreading. A woman's false eyelashes leave her with abrasions to her eyes(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-two-year-old retired firefighter John is airlifted to A&E after being accidentally hit in the face with a golf club, which hit him so hard that it snapped. With a suspected critical head injury, he is sent for a CT scan, and doctors are concerned that his right eye is not reacting as it should and he is weak down the right-hand side of his body. Meanwhile, 77-year-old Joan arrives in St George's after falling down the stairs at home and hurting her leg. As she waits to be treated, she talks about growing up in Camberwell(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The cleric tries to nurture Chris, the hot favourite to win the King of the Sheep festival, back to top form after he is left shaken by a terrifying encounter. Meanwhile, Dougal becomes convinced Craggy Island is being terrorised by a monster(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal takes over as Craggy Island's new milkman, but his predecessor, local womaniser Pat Mustard, returns to exact an explosive revenge on Ted for getting him sacked. Starring Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan(n)

programme.title: It Was Alright In
programme.desc: With attitudes to feminism, foreigners and family values being somewhat different 40 years ago, the second of two programmes narrated by Matt Lucas focuses on 'good old-fashioned Britishness' on TV in the 1970s. From blacking-up in popular pre-watershed programmes, through to homophobia and xenophobia, the decade seems to have had a different set of manners and morals, and took a casual approach to sexism and racism. Looking back on a selection of surprising clips are the people who made the shows, those who watched them at the time, and younger viewers providing a fresh perspective(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson provide their irreverent take on the cosy afternoon puzzle game - showing a little less regard for the rules. Comedian Bob Mortimer and Pointless co-host Richard Osman are the guest players, while Countdown regular Susie Dent is in Dictionary Corner with funnyman Matthew Crosby, and Joe Wilkinson 'assists' maths whizz Rachel Riley with the letters and numbers(n)

programme.title: Savage Kingdom
programme.desc: Three lionesses living in an area of forests and hills face the end of their pride from starvation after losing their male cubs(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

Kate Quilton goes in search of the perfect tomato and finds herself in the middle of the world's biggest food fight at a festival in Valencia, and Matt Tebbutt examines why tinned anchovies taste salty when fresh ones don't, visiting a processing plant to find out(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect and presenter revisits some of the finest builds from across Europe, with an epic mountain-top retreat in the Italian Alps and a glass-walled lake house in Lugano. There is also an ingenious bed in Gelnhausen, Germany and a floating home in Denmark partly made out of recycled debris. Plus, in Barcelona, George visits possibly the smallest city home he's ever seen - a 24-square-metre roof top studio apartment where everything is hidden in the walls(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets a father-of-three building a campervan for less than £700, made from old CDs and vinyl records, and a single dad who is renovating an oil-rig escape pod to make a home for himself and sons on the water. The presenter also continues his German road trip with a visit to the Tyrolean mountains, where he finds a house that looks like a cross between a large bird and a UFO, and he and Will Hardie save a decrepit old wartime lookout shed to use as the basis for their floating beach hut(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: This time, Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are house-hunting in and around Bristol. First, Kirstie's with Craig and Rich, who have been looking for a home to start a family in for over a year. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Outgoing hair salon owner Natalie Crompton hosts the second dinner party in Wigan, and since she loves to party she plans a fun-filled night for her guests - including some very special treats(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

He wants his 'posh' menu to dazzle his guests, but salon owner Natalie Crompton is worried about some of the food on offer. The other contestants also discover his secret lifelong passion for ferreting(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

However, hospital theatre manager Geoff Conway is on the verge of being thrown out if he continues his run of misbehaviour. At the end of proceedings the winner of the £1,000 is revealed(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Alston House in Cumbria, where owners Michael and Carole Allchorne are brimming with confidence, but Yvonne Copeland and Gail Wells are less than impressed with the decor. The guests are impressed with chef Michael's breakfast, but the hosts are left reeling by the criticisms levelled at their hotel during the feedback session(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Scotia House in Harrogate, where host Marie Lock is hoping her obsessive cleaning regime will give her the edge in the competition. In the rooms, both Michael and Carole and Yvonne and Gail find the deluxe teddy bears freaky, and for Billy and Billy, things are a little on the cosy side. In the afternoon, Marie's keen to show off her moves and pits her guests against each other in a Northern Soul dance off(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Canberra Guest House in Blackpool for the third day of the competition, where host Yvonne Copeland, joined by her part-time helper Gail Wells, hopes her cheap and cheerful B&B will win over her guests. However, Marie finds what she thinks is urine and hair on the toilet seat in her room, and a lack of wi-fi means Billy Junior has to put his business on hold. Yvonne takes her competitors to a local reptile house, but serious divisions unfold within the group, and later, tensions rise again at dinner(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Crown and Kitchen in East Linton, East Lothian, owned by father and son Billy and Billy Jr Robertson, who see themselves as the jokers of the pack. Yvonne and Gail leave no mattress unturned in their room inspection, although Michael and Carole are impressed. Later, Yvonne makes quite a splash when the hosts organise an assault course for their guests, but slow service during breakfast leaves the B&Bers disappointed - especially when Billy Jr is at a loss to explain what the problem was(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. Things are tense from the start as Carole and Michael from Alston House challenge Marie on her comments. Next, Marie from Scotia House wants to set the record straight with allegations of tit-for-tat scoring. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Surrey, five cooks from the Woking and Weybridge area battle to win the culinary contest's £1,000 prize. The first to host is Vincenzo Ferlita, who works in an ice-cream van. Staying true to his Italian and Sicilian roots, he hopes that his wealth of family recipes will impress his guests(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Canadian housewife and craft-maker Sarah Maynard is second to play host in the Woking and Weybridge area, and hopes to impress her guests by serving up a three-course Turkish feast. However, the lavish spread needs a lot of preparation time, and she must hope it turns out as presentable as she plans it to be(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Riot policeman Steve Carvill hosts the third dinner party in Woking and Weybridge in Surrey, and hopes that a night of fine food and fun will impress his guests. He takes no prisoners with his menu, rustling up home-made pasta and white chocolate panna cotta from scratch for his four unsuspecting diners, who are expecting fast food rather than fine dining(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the turn of gardener Sara Gilding on the fourth night in Woking and Weybridge in Surrey, but it is her first time hosting a dinner party in more than 20 years. Guest Vincenzo Ferlita is not looking forward to the evening, while Steve Carvill hopes he will get some respite from Sara's flirtatious advances(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Children's entertainer Kelly Cummins hosts the final party from Woking and Weybridge in Surrey, and the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced. The guests keep their fingers crossed that dog-loving Sara Gilding does not return to her critical ways, while a bout of flatulence provides some comic relief(n)

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Helen is trapped in her car after a crash on the A66, while golfer Michael has collapsed with a suspected cardiac arrest in Hertfordshire. Plus, 15-year-old Jamie is treated for a broken jaw and possible spinal injury after falling 10 feet(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedic Rob and technician Mike rush to a 21-month-old baby who has fallen unconscious in Kidderminster, while two men collapse in a pub in Coventry(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: After falling over during the night at home and being rushed into A&E, 85-year-old Maura faces potentially life-changing injuries. CT scans reveal broken bones in her spine, and her son and daughter-in-law must decide when to share the gravity of the situation with Maura and her granddaughter Meg. Meanwhile, 37-year-old doctor Jim arrives at St George's after a collision with a van while he was cycling to work. Despite putting on a brave face, scans reveal his injuries are more serious than first thought. Plus, a chef is treated after cutting her thumb while chopping peppers at home(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Over the last decade, incidents of violence perpetrated by young men have risen by 22 per cent and this edition examines the stories behind some of these calls and crimes. In Swindon, officers Emily Grigor and Simon Sanghera are called to arrest a man in his early twenties who has ripped off his top in a drunken rage before putting his fist through a wall and his foot through a window in a bar(n)

programme.title: 24 Hour Baby Hospital
programme.desc: Behind closed doors with parents and staff at The Rotunda in Dublin - the world's longest-running maternity hospital, where more than a million Dubliners have been born. Accompanied by her mother Kay, Nicola arrives at the labour ward, hoping for the safe arrival of baby number four, Larkin(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedic Rob and technician Mike rush to a 21-month-old baby who has fallen unconscious in Kidderminster, while two men collapse in a pub in Coventry(n)

programme.title: Savage Kingdom
programme.desc: Animals battle for domination of hunting grounds in a remote region of Africa, focusing on a lioness defending her territory against rival prides(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits the largest wholesale food market in the world in Paris and discovers why British cheese sales are soaring across Europe. Matt Tebbutt travels to one of the largest ham producers in Italy and meets a pig farmer who tells him about a delicate problem that all pork producers face, while Kate Quilton investigates why some limes are so much juicier than others(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty looks into skyr, a mysterious yoghurt-like product that claims to be entirely fat-free, but has a surprisingly creamy texture, and Kate Quilton heads to one of the busiest oyster hatcheries in France, where she witnesses the amazing science that goes into providing oysters year-round. Matt Tebbutt wants to know if the dried strawberries in his cereal are real or just a clever imitation(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Presenters Jonnie Irwin and Laura Hamilton help a couple in their quest for a change of scenery. Cardiff pair Graham and Sally Jenkins have been together for 40 years, but they are unable to decide whether they should set up a new home in west Wiltshire, or Martel in the Lot region of south-western France(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Celia Thompson has long dreamed of running an arts and crafts business in the Carcassonne area of France. However, her partner Nick Hares is hesitant about leaving the UK, as his teenage daughters live near Cambridge. He'd rather move to the Waveney Valley, on the border of Suffolk and Norfolk. Jonnie Irwin and Laura Hamilton help the couple solve their dilemma(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the Cimon in Torquay, Devon, where owners Mark and Jo Warden hope their four-star establishment will impress their guests(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to The Cardinal's Hat in Worcester, where owner Nigel Smith has decades of hospitality experience, but Mark and Jo are concerned by the condition of the bedding(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Third to host this week are husband and wife duo Russ and Karen Colman, who hope their glamping site in Cledan Valley, mid-Wales, will win over their rivals(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lucinda Renshaw welcomes the guests to Springfield House B&B in Instow, Devon, where things get off to a good start - until one couple realise they do not have a lock on their door(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. The Cimon is first and hosts Mark and Jo want to question Karen and Russ about their breakfast feedback. Next, Nigel from The Cardinal's Hat wants clarity on some cleaning and hygiene comments made by Mark and Jo. Russ and Karen from Cledan Valley are third and are keen to put Mark and Jo straight. Finally, it's Springfield House and owner Lucinda and friend Diane want to put two of the guests at ease, but it's the payments where true feelings are expressed(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Digital account manager Alice hosts the first of a week of dinner parties from in and around Leeds, hoping her family favourites will hit the spot and win her the £1,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Night two of the competition in Leeds, and company director Andy hopes to win with a menu of restaurant-inspired fare, but Alice and Danielle are more impressed with his Ferrari(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fun-loving student Danielle hosts the third dinner party in Leeds, hoping a high-calorie menu will see her emerge victorious. She even serves dessert wearing her wedding dress(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theatre technician Matt is the fourth to host in Leeds with a menu of family favourites - and if that does not win them over, hopefully his juggling skills will do the trick(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final day of the competition in Leeds, and jeweller Lynne tries to rescue her dinner party with a memorable karaoke performance(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are in Northamptonshire with Isabel and Louis Prat, whose three decades of marital bliss is being shaken by their house. The former social worker and nurse are desperate to enjoy their retirement in their dream home, but while Louis is passionate about the abode and the local area, Isabel hates their property. With a refurb budget of £70,000, Kirstie is confident she can give Isabel the ground floor space she wants, while Phil takes to the road with the couple to show them the amazing homes they could buy(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A woman brings in Teddy the shih-tzu cross, who has severely deformed front legs. His owner wants him to live a life without pain, but coming up with a surgical solution proves a challenge. Five-year-old golden retriever Ollie is referred to Fitzpatrick's for an operation to remove a tumour in his leg, and a young couple bring in their four-month-old bulldog, which suffered a fractured elbow after wriggling from their arms(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of private chef Ed Versluys and pilates instructor Vicky Anderson, who want to convert a concrete cowshed in the Somerset countryside into a three bedroom home. With the help of one young builder and the knowledge they can learn from the internet, the couple plan to project manage the conversion themselves. However, they have only eight months and a budget of just over £200k to make a warm and comfortable home with straw bale walls and wide expanses of glass(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As autumn sets in, Dick and Angel harvest their pumpkins in the walled garden and organise a family wheelbarrow race. The basement is the latest room to be transformed, serving as a nerve centre for the whole chateau, a space for storing deliveries, and Dick's workshop. The old piggery is also renovated, in anticipation of some new arrivals(n)

programme.title: A Royal Tour of the 20th Century
programme.desc: A look at official tours and state visits in the 1960s, exploring the changing role of the royal family's relationship to the Commonwealth and the political upheaval in the world(n)

programme.title: It Was Alright In
programme.desc: Matt Lucas narrates another look back at TV shows from past decades, with clips and reactions to programmes from people not around to watch them at the time. This edition revisits the changing social attitudes reflected on the small screen in the 1960s, including humorous shows which took liberties in the name of satire, disability being mocked and bulletproof vests being put to the test on live TV. With contributions by Joan Bakewell, Peter Purves, Valerie Singleton and Melvyn Bragg(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Train driver Andy, 48, is rushed in after falling 12ft from a tree while cutting branches and landing on his feet, badly fracturing both ankles, and doctors are concerned the impact may have caused life-changing damage to critical nerves and blood vessels in his feet and legs. Angela, 59, has come to St George's as her heart has been beating at over 150bpm for eight hours, risking stroke and other complications. Cardiologist Dr Arun's attempts to bring down her pulse using a powerful drug aren't successful, so he decides to electrically shock her heart to reset it(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May immerse themselves in nostalgia and try to prove that hot hatches from their younger days are better than today's equivalents. Jeremy grabs the keys to a Volkswagen Golf GTI, Richard takes the wheel of a Vauxhall Nova SRi and James May opts for the Ford Fiesta XR2i, and the trio set off on a road trip that includes a hill climb and a visit to a supermarket. However, their adventure concludes at an army training ground in Wales, where they and their steeds must face the heavily armed Top Gear Police Department. Downton Abbey star Hugh Bonneville blasts the Reasonably Priced Car around the test track(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The trio travel to Italy to test high-performance hatchbacks. Jeremy Clarkson is in the Citroen DS3 Racing, Richard Hammond champions the Fiat 500C Abarth and James May makes the case for the Renaultsport Clio 200 Cup as they navigate their way round baffling Italian towns and take part in a scavenger hunt, before putting the cars through their paces on the F1 street circuit in Monaco. Also, petrolhead comedian Ross Noble is in the Reasonably Priced Car and the McLaren MP4-12C supercar comes under review(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Hal Cruttenden, Thomas Turgoose, Lucy Porter, Ainsley Harriott, Chris Ramsey, Nick Helm, Anna Crilly and Katy Wix looks for bargains in Caxton, Cambridgeshire(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank go on the road in search for a unique motorbike, only to receive some shocking news from an appraiser(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank explore a disused shop, and visit a collector's garage where they find a motorcycle frame, a go-kart and some rare toys(n)

programme.title: Steve Backshall's Extreme River Challenge
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part programme, the naturalist travels down the Baliem River's lower gorge, one of the most dangerous stretches of white water in the world surrounded by jagged rock walls. His exploration of the surrounding jungle leads to an encounter with a tribe that has had little contact with the outside world. As he concludes his trek in the river's coastal reaches, he faces a crisis of conscience as he witnesses a traditional crocodile hunt(n)

programme.title: Cops UK: Bodycam Squad
programme.desc: Complaints by neighbours about anti-social behaviour leads to a raid on a suspected drugs den in Burton. Documentary series following the work of the Staffordshire Police force(n)

programme.title: Mega Air

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond find second-hand bargains for the same price as the £6,995 Nissan Pixo. James May is in Nevada to test the toughness of the Range Rover Evoque, driving it through Death Valley before chauffeuring a megastar across Las Vegas. Plus, the McLaren MP4-12C is compared with its supercar rival the Ferrari 458 Italia, and four-time Formula One world champion Sebastian Vettel is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the mid-engined Alfa Romeo 4C on the shores of Lake Como in Italy - but his enjoyment of the lightweight vehicle is disturbed by the arrival of Jeremy Clarkson, who demands a race against his quad bike. Jeremy also visits Belgium, putting the McLaren P1 hypercar to the test in Bruges and around the Spa-Francorchamps Formula One circuit - and its combined power output of more than 900bhp generated from electricity and turbocharged petrol V8 power is likely to cause astonishment. Meanwhile, James May spends time at Camp Bastion in Afghanistan to learn about the army's vehicle redeployment efforts, and Thor star Tom Hiddleston tries to coax the best time he can muster from the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.desc: Seven years into their marriage, Lee and Lucy face the daily onslaught of raising Charlie and his twin siblings, five-year-olds Benji and Molly. Lovably feckless father Frank remains around and close friends including world-weary Toby and uptight ice-queen Anna are still on the scene. programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcock's films and Graham Greene's books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide(n)

One of the people he saved, Renee Harris, gifted him a set of gold-plated nautical equipment as a symbol of her gratitude. programme.title: History's Ultimate Spies
programme.desc: After the death of Cardinal Wolsey in 1530, Thomas Cromwell became Henry VIII's 'lap dog'. In order to do the king's bidding, he established a network of spies that steered Henry through his troubles with Rome, divorces, assassinations attempts and the establishment of a new religion. as this documentary reveals, though cunning and ambitious, none of these achievements proved sufficient to preserve his life(n)

programme.title: History's Ultimate Spies
programme.desc: As a Protestant, Queen Elizabeth I lived with the threat of assassination from Catholics throughout her reign. Thankfully, there was an army of men available to work in secret under spy master Francis Walsingham with the sole aim of protecting her. As this documentary reveals, Walsingham was able to demonstrate remarkable cunning in his role(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on an expedition along Dorset's Jurassic coast to explore the county's hidden Second World War story. Starting beside the defences on Chesil Beach, he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay. On his five-day walk he encounters stunning scenery and uncovers wartime acts of ingenuity and bravery. He also discovers the crucial part a swannery played in developing a secret weapon and reveals the role of Dorset in protecting Britain from invasion during the conflict(n)

programme.desc: David Attenborough observes the wildlife of the desert-like west and south of the island, an area where dry spells can last months and survival is a struggle. Verreaux's sifakas live among trees with potentially lethal spikes, while the baobab trees house mouse lemurs, primates that feed on the droppings of fluffy bugs. During the toughest time of year, gangs of ring-tailed lemurs try to raise their young, and as the rains arrive, the year-long life cycle of the Labord's chameleon begins(n)

programme.title: Frozen Planet

He also discovers how the Danish armed forces maintain their country's claim to Greenland's natural resources by mounting a 2,000-mile dog sled patrol during the winter, and finds out how a research base at the South Pole is able to cope with being cut off from the outside world for six months at a time(n)

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three previous restaurants he tried to save - Handlebar, Casa Roma and the Black Pearl - to find out whether his advice has been put to good use(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke and master craftsman Will Hardie embark on a road trip to explore the ingenious small-space wonders hidden away among the snowy peaks of the Swiss, French and Italian Alps. They start out from an observation point 4,000 metres above sea level, then take in a secret ski lodge disguised as a boulder, a treehouse resembling a giant acorn and a Swiss cabin made almost entirely from concrete. Other highlights include a hamlet made from eight 200-year-old chalets collected from all over the Alps and a mountaintop restaurant resembling a spaceship(n)

programme.title: Great British Car Journeys

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie and Alan hope to cheer up a depressed Evelyn by inviting Russell the pharmacist to her birthday party. However, when he shows up with a hot date in tow, the Harper matriarch ends up even more upset(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Bertha takes a few days off and arranges for a friend to fill in as a replacement housekeeper, but she proves to be so good at her work that Charlie has to choose between the two. programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan feels inadequate when he realises he cannot shower Lindsey with gifts the way Charlie does with Courtney. Desperate to gain ground, he offers massages at the mall for extra money, but when this venture fails to bring in a decent profit, he decides to resort to desperate measures(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan forbids Lindsey from inviting Charlie to her housewarming party, convinced his womanising brother is about to make a move on her. However, the plan backfires and he ends up getting banned himself - forcing him to spy on proceedings from the outside(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan receives investment money from family members for his booming chiropractic business, and pockets their contribution instead of using it to further his plans. Meanwhile, Gordon delivers a pizza to Rose's house, and catches sight of her new husband(n)

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords
programme.desc: Updates on stories featured in previous episodes, including Kent couple Rachel and Chris, who were pushed to the limit when their relationship with their tenant broke down and she stopped paying rent, leaving them more than £5,000 out of pocket. Plus, a young mum locked out of her home by the young couple she allowed to stay there for free while the wife had a baby, and a landlord who reduced her tenants' rent when they got into financial difficulties - only for the payments to end altogether(n)

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords
programme.desc: Two ministers in Liverpool take over an old church with a plan to turn it into a community centre, only to be obstructed by a group of squatters who refuse to leave. Time to call in eviction expert Chris Sharpe. Council officers Shital and Andrew are on the warpath when a neglectful landlord ignores their warnings about fire safety. Northamptonshire landlord Sina visits her property to ask why payments had stopped, only to be faced by an angry and volatile tenant, leaving her scared to return(n)

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords
programme.desc: The documentary about landlord and tenancy issues catches up on some of the stories featured so far. Emmanuel reveals what happened after he returned from a year working abroad to find himself £24,000 in mortgage arrears and unable to get into his own property. Andy made a mistake when he rented out his north London home to someone he knew, only for his so-called friend to sublet the property, pocket the profits and pay Andy nothing. What has happened since?(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: In Bristol, eviction lawyer Chris deals with a complex case in which squatters have broken into an empty pub and are now refusing to leave, with landlord Alex wanting them out as he is awaiting planning permission to turn the pub into flats. Jill is trying to regain a property she and her late husband bought as an investment, which she has been renting out to alleviate financial stress(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul Bohill and Steve and Ben Pinner face their biggest eviction yet - taking on squatters in a large central London office block. With the help of the police, they try to remove a suspected group of 70 from the building, but with a barricade in place, the team faces a challenging operation ahead. Elsewhere, officers are confronted by an army of family and friends when they attempt to enforce a writ at the property of a debtor, and face a difficult eviction at the north London home of a heavily pregnant tenant and her family, after the council fails to pay the rent to the landlord(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul and Steve make an extraordinary discovery inside a locked bedroom, and immediately call for police back-up. Brian and Graham head to Swindon Town football club to recover a debt, while Paul and Ben have difficulty evicting a couple from a house in Kent after learning they have six children and nowhere else to go(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary giving an insight into cash-strapped Britain from the perspectives of debtors, creditors and those who collect the payments, including High Court enforcement bailiffs and repossession teams. Paul and Ben are called in to evict squatters from a residential property, but something about the squat does not quite add up for the team(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul and Steve's first job is an eviction with a twist when they find out the landlord is the tenant's son, while their next case is also tricky and involves family members with a question mark over their immigration status who are now facing homelessness. Their final case involves a man with a history of mental illness who has reached the end of his tenancy but refuses to leave, and Steve tries to find a solution after a church minister and his mother fail to placate him(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine visits the home of his former captain, only to find the man has been killed, and the killer is waiting inside the property. While the soldier escapes with his life, the attacker gets away too. It quickly emerges the prime suspect is Qasim, an Afghan translator evidence, and evidence points against him, until it is discovered he has a brother, who is loyal to the Taliban. The subsequent chain of evidence leads Gibbs and Bishop to Afghanistan(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Naval intelligence ensign is killed in an unusual car accident. Gibbs and his team are reunited with old friend Ned Dorneget, who is now working with NCIS cyber operations, and NSA attorney Jake Malloy to investigate the case, and discover a conspiracy involving terrorist recruitment on the internet(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Rollins and Benson investigate the rape of an underage girl, who initially refuses to co-operate or press charges against a trio of suspects, but eventually comes clean and leads the detectives to the strip club where she works - although her boss denies any wrongdoing and rejects a request for his client list. Starring Mariska Hargitay and Kelli Giddish(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team responds to the reported kidnapping of a 16-year-old girl at a party - and her disappearance could be linked with her boyfriend's gang associations. Elsewhere, Benson has a ceremony to decree her becoming the new SVU sergeant. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team members search for a missing diabetic child who also suffers from an attachment disorder. A shocking confession from the youngster's mother leads them to a couple of known sex offenders, and the detectives face a race against time to find the kid before he falls into a coma. Meanwhile, Benson is preoccupied by a pregnancy scare. Guest starring Rosanna Arquette(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A controversial comedian must defend both his act and his actions when he is accused of inciting the assault of a protester and raping one of his fans. Benson struggles to get the jury to believe his victims, until one of them takes matters into her own hands. Guest starring Jonathan Silverman, Elizabeth Marvel and Skyler Day(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A reformed gang member claims he wants to make amends for past misdeeds by helping Horatio solve the riddle of why a kind-hearted community worker from a crime-ridden neighbourhood was buried alive. The offer seems genuine, and is gratefully accepted - but doubts remain about his true motives. Drama, starring David Caruso(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team suspects a former jockey and a jealous investor of murdering a wealthy racehorse owner. Meanwhile, Delko grows increasingly attracted to Calleigh - but learns she might have another admirer. David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez star(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami

Crime drama from The Silence of the Lambs director Jonathan Demme, starring Peter Falk, Louis Jourdan, Shera Danese and Richard Dysart(n)

programme.desc: An orchestra conductor murders his mistress for threatening to reveal their affair, and makes her death look like suicide. However, he reckons without the intervention of crumpled cop Columbo, who sees through the subterfuge and determines to get to the truth. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, John Cassavetes, Myrna Loy and Blythe Danner(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Dagger of the Mind (1972)
programme.desc: A London theatre producer discovers his actress wife is having an affair with her co-star in their latest production. To avoid exposure, the pair murder him and make it look as if his death was an accident. Meanwhile, Columbo is in the city to pick up some tips from Scotland Yard, and sets about uncovering the truth. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Honor Blackman, Richard Basehart and Wilfrid Hyde White(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate the death of an education adviser whose body has been found in the East River - a case apparently connected to a series of substantial gambling losses he had run up but was hiding from his wife. At first it appears to have been a suicide, but when it is revealed his body was covered in anti-seasickness patches to induce drowsiness, it becomes clear it was murder(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The agents are alerted to an armoured-car heist in which five men stole $70million in gold bars and killed two armed guards in the process. When Callen and the team investigate, they encounter the dead bodies of all but one of the robbers along with the entire seized haul - but is their luck too good to be true? Meanwhile, Granger partners up with a reluctant Kensi. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam, Kensi and Deeks cancel their Christmas plans and jet off to East Asia to investigate the death of a fellow NCIS special agent. Meanwhile, back at headquarters, festive celebrations are getting under way as Nell and Eric make the most of the mistletoe lying around, and Hetty reveals that she is a real softie at heart. Drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Starring Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek(n)

Detective drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin(n)

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: With more than half of the UK's adults are currently overweight, Cambridge geneticist Dr Giles Yeo believes that for many obese people, simply eating less is a harder than some might think. He takes a road trip around the UK and America to uncover why, meets people behind some of the shocking newspaper headlines, and through their stories, reveals truths which dispel commonly held myths about obesity(n)

programme.title: The Thirty-Nine Steps (1978)
programme.desc: An innocent man is informed of a devious plan to start the First World War by murdering a foreign minister. Almost immediately, he is framed for murder and embarks on a perilous cross-country trek to prove his innocence, pursued by both the police and ruthless spies. Thriller - the third film adaptation of John Buchan's novel - starring Robert Powell, Karen Dotrice, John Mills and George Baker(n)

programme.title: Welcome to the Mosque
programme.desc: Robb Leech provides an insight into what life is like behind a mosque's walls, from rituals and marriage, to racism experienced by older community members when they were growing up. He witnesses segregation and meets younger members of the community, who are afraid of 'free-mixing' with the opposite sex. He also captures the response to the news of the three London schoolgirls who left Britain to join Isis jihadists in Syria, including frantic phone calls and the journey to Istanbul with the girls' families to find them(n)

programme.title: Strictly Confidential (1959)
programme.desc: Two conmen fresh out of prison are given prestigious jobs by a widow - not realising she has a devious scheme in mind. Comedy, starring Richard Murdoch, William Kendall, William Hartnell and Maya Koumani(n)

programme.title: A Handful of Dust (1989)
programme.desc: A couple's life is disrupted by a penniless woman who arrives for the weekend, setting in motion a disastrous sequence of events. Drama, starring Judi Dench, whose performance won her a Bafta award, and James Wilby(n)

programme.title: Filthy Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow heads for 18th-century Paris to discover how the insanitary living conditions endured by its poorest residents helped bring about the French Revolution. He visits a perfumer to re-create some of the city's unsavoury smells, and finds out what happened to the thousands of bodies that overflowed from cemeteries during the Terror of 1793. He also tries his hand at tanning leather the traditional way - using dog faeces and urine(n)

programme.title: The Coronation of Queen Elizabeth II
programme.desc: Documentary charting the historic events of June 2, 1953, when the Queen became the 39th sovereign to be crowned at Westminster Abbey. What happened behind the scenes is explored through diaries, letters and government papers, as well as rare film footage and photographs, and there are also interviews with historians and experts on royal ceremonies. The programme reveals that Her Majesty's apparent calmness during the Coronation contrasted with organisers' last-minute nerves as the BBC mounted its most ambitious television broadcast to date, amid the concerns of prime minister Winston Churchill(n)

programme.title: Heyday TV
programme.desc: The leading members of the Kinks who were in the band from 1965 through to 1985 recall their time recording and touring(n)

programme.title: Redemption (2013)
programme.desc: A haunted special forces soldier returns to London from Afghanistan and assumes another man's identity, hoping to make a fresh start. He is drawn into the city's criminal underworld, where his skills prove valuable to the highest bidder, and plots revenge against the gangster who killed his girlfriend. Crime thriller, with Jason Statham and Agata Buzek(n)

programme.title: Hallows Eve (2016)
programme.desc: Shadowy figures in masks begin appearing around an estate with a reputation for street crime, and leave behind graffiti warning of their intent to take over the area on Halloween. The members of a street gang vow to take a stand against the invaders, but are unprepared for the shocking truth behind their enemies' masks. Horror, starring Sarah Akokhia and Ethan Taylor(n)

programme.title: Run
programme.desc: A Polish cleaner living in London discovers her gambling addict boyfriend has been killed in a vicious attack. She then unearths his secret relationship with another woman, and finds herself in grave danger. The last of four dramas telling the stories of seemingly unconnected people facing life-changing decisions, starring Katharina Schuttler, Jaime Winstone and Marcin Dorocinski(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: With more than half of the UK's adults are currently overweight, Cambridge geneticist Dr Giles Yeo believes that for many obese people, simply eating less is a harder than some might think. He takes a road trip around the UK and America to uncover why, meets people behind some of the shocking newspaper headlines, and through their stories, reveals truths which dispel commonly held myths about obesity(n)

programme.desc: Punk singer Chris Sweeney takes up the conductor's baton as he prepares to pass himself off as the leader of a classical orchestra, but with no ability to read music or play an instrument, he faces an uphill struggle to master the necessary skills in only four weeks(n)

Mystery, based on Edgar Wallace's novel Sanders on the River, starring Richard Todd, Marianne Koch, Albert Lieven and Walter Rilla(n)

programme.title: Time Without Pity (1957)
programme.desc: An alcoholic writer returns home after years living abroad when he learns his estranged son has been convicted of murder and sentenced to hang. Although his attempts at reconciliation are rejected, he spends the last 24 hours before the execution trying to prove his son is innocent by identifying the real killer. Mystery, starring Michael Redgrave, Ann Todd, Leo McKern and Peter Cushing(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the history of Windsor Castle, which has evolved over more than 1,000 years from its original role as a fortress and was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1992. Inside the residence is a musket ball that killed a naval hero, jewel-encrusted gold and silver table decorations, and Queen Mary's doll's house - complete with running water and a secret garden(n)

programme.title: Our Queen
programme.desc: Feature-length documentary filmed throughout 2012, providing an in-depth portrait of Elizabeth II during her Diamond Jubilee year. Prince Charles and other members of the royal family share their stories, providing insights into the Queen's life over an important and busy 12 months filled with celebrations. The programme also reveals what really happens when the prime minister sits down with the monarch, and the lid is lifted on that memorable 007 moment at the London Olympic Games opening ceremony(n)

programme.title: No Stranger Than Love (2015)
programme.desc: A married woman has an affair with a co-worker, only for him to be sucked into a mysterious hole that suddenly appears in her home. She has to find a way to get him out of this strange imprisonment without anyone learning about their secret relationship. Sci-fi romantic comedy, starring Alison Brie and Justin Chatwin(n)
